title: Not Like Us
lyrics:
[Intro]
Psst, I see dead people
  > [#1, 625 votes] In The Sixth Sense (1999), the film’s protagonist, Cole Sear (potrayed by Haley Joel Osment), can see and communicate with dead people and whispers the same words that the boy utters to his...
(Mustard on the beat, ho)
  > [#2, 536 votes] Los Angeles-based producer Mustard’s signature producer tag is an excerpt of frequent collaborator and Compton artist YG that originated from YG’s 2011 track “I’m Good.” This is notable because Drake...

[Verse 1]
Ayy, Mustard on the beat, ho
  > [#2, see above]
Deebo any rap nigga, he a free throw
  > [#3, 339 votes] Deebo, portrayed by Compton actor Tommy Lister Jr., is a fictional character from the iconic 1995 film Friday. He is depicted as a sociopathic bully that no one in the community is willing to stand...
Man down, call an amberlamps, tell him, "Breathe, bro"
  > [#4, 199 votes] “Man down” is a military term utilized when a soldier is injured during combat. Kendrick likens this to Drake losing the rap battle. “Amberlamps" is a reference to the 2010 AC Transit bus fight,...
Nail a nigga to the cross, he walk around like Teezo
  > [#5, 405 votes] Kendrick likens his verbal annihilation of Drake to a crucifixion, which traditionally involves the nailing or fastening of an individual to a cross — two perpendicular blocks of wood. The line could...
What's up with these jabroni-ass niggas tryna see Compton?
  > [#6, 295 votes] Born and raised in Compton, Lamar is pointing out his frustration with Drake’s constant antagonizations that he isn’t scared of coming to his hometown. Drake has mentioned that he gets more love than...
The industry can hate me, fuck 'em all and they mama
  > [#7, 224 votes] Kendrick unapologetically declares his intention to confront Drake, regardless of industry alliances. He acknowledges the potential repercussions, but doubles down on sentiments conveyed on April...
How many opps you really got? I mean, it's too many options
I'm finna pass on this body, I'm John Stockton
  > [#8, 463 votes] John Stockton is a legendary basketballer for Utah Jazz in the NBA who holds the record for most assists in NBA history with 15,806. Stockton famously played much of his career alongside power...
Beat your ass and hide the Bible if God watchin'
  > [#9, 325 votes] K-Dot makes it clear that he will put aside his well-established Christian beliefs and values if he has to hurt Drake or anyone else. This bar bears resemblance to some wordplay found on April 2017’s...
Sometimes you gotta pop out and show niggas
  > [#10, 242 votes] Kendrick calls back to a line from Drake’s April 2024 diss track, “Taylor Made Freestyle,” rapped from the perspective of an AI-generated Tupac Shakur: You supposed to be the boogeyman, go do what...
Certified boogeyman, I'm the one that up the score with 'em
Walk him down, whole time, I know he got some ho in him
  > [#11, 160 votes] In street slang, “walking down” refers to approaching an enemy on foot before shooting them, as opposed to shooting at them while driving by in a car. In boxing, walking down an opponent refers to a...
Pole on him, extort shit, bully Death Row on him
  > [#12, 195 votes] On the surface, death row refers to the place where criminals await execution or capital punishment. However, here, it doubles as a nod to Death Row Records, the infamous label founded by the...
Say, Drake, I hear you like 'em young
  > [#13, 480 votes] Kendrick directly interpolates an old Drake tweet from 2009 where he declares that he’s sleeping with someone so young he should be going to jail. Kendrick continues the accusations of Drake’s...
You better not ever go to cell block one
  > [#14, 306 votes] Doubling-down on the sexual misconduct allegations, Kendrick is insinuating that Drake should be imprisoned as a result. The prison system can be a menacing place for child molesters/sexual...
To any bitch that talk to him and they in love
  > [#15, 275 votes] Kendrick doubles down on calling Drake a pedophile, this time warning his potential lovers. Drake has stirred controversy over his involvement with underage girls; a video has even surfaced of him...
Just make sure you hide your lil' sister from him
They tell me Chubbs the only one that get your hand-me-downs
  > [#16, 120 votes] This line is based upon two differing usages of the phrase “hand-me-downs.” Literally, a “hand-me-down” refers to a possession that is no longer wanted or needed and this is offered to to someone...
And Party at the party playin' with his nose now
  > [#17, 306 votes] Kendrick mocks the drug use—specifically cocaine—of OVO singer and songwriter PARTYNEXTDOOR. He was arrested in 2017 while attempting to cross the US-Canada border in possession of Xanax and...
And Baka got a weird case, why is he around?
  > [#18, 482 votes] Travis Savoury, known as Baka Not Nice, was previously a former security guard who signed with Drake’s record label, OVO Sound, an imprint of Warner Records from 2017. In 2014, Baka was arrested and...
Certified Lover Boy? Certified pedophiles
  > [#19, 780 votes] Certified Lover Boy is the title of Drake’s sixth studio album, which he released on September 3, 2021. His use of the word “pedophile” stems from Drake’s history of repeatedly allegedly grooming and...
Wop, wop, wop, wop, wop, Dot, fuck 'em up
  > [#20, 224 votes] The “wop” onomatopoeia mimics the sound of a flurry of punches thrown in quick succession. Kendrick is overwhelming Drake with his lyrical jabs. The adlib delivery mirrors the 2010 club hit “Wop” by...
Wop, wop, wop, wop, wop, I'ma do my stuff
Why you trollin' like a bitch? Ain't you tired?
  > [#21, 1431 votes] To “strike a chord” means to affect or stir someone’s emotions. This is likely a reference to Drake posting memes about Kendrick throughout their feud, but may also refer to his April 2024 diss...
Tryna strike a chord and it's probably A minor

[Chorus]
They not like us, they not like us, they not like us
  > [#22, 346 votes] Kendrick comments on how Drake (and the people he surrounds himself with) are “not like us,” saying that anybody that Kendrick surrounds himself with would not replicate the behaviours that he refers...
They not like us, they not like us, they not like us

[Verse 2]
You think the Bay gon' let you disrespect Pac, nigga?
  > [#23, 417 votes] In the first line, Kendrick criticizes Drake for mimicking deceased rapper 2Pac’s voice using artificial intelligence on his second Kendrick diss track “Taylor Made Freestyle.” Less than a week after...
I think that Oakland show gon' be your last stop, nigga
Did Cole foul, I don't know why you still pretendin'
  > [#24, 436 votes] Kendrick may be referring to three things here: He’s saying Drake did J. Cole dirty by bringing him onto his song, “First Person Shooter” and allowing him to think that he, Drake, and Kendrick were...
What is the owl? Bird niggas and burnt bitches, go
  > [#25, 152 votes] Drake is the founder and owner of OVO Sound record label. OVO stands for October’s Very Own and its logo is an owl, as a nod to the way the letters “OVO” resemble an owl’s face. Kendrick plays on...
The audience not dumb
  > [#26, 184 votes] Drake has been trying to use his fame and larger fan base to shape the narrative around this conflict. For instance, Lamar alleged that Drake tried to cease and desist “Like That” in the lyrics of...
Shape the stories how you want, hey, Drake, they're not slow
Rabbit hole is still deep, I can go further, I promise
  > [#27, 213 votes] “Going down a rabbit hole” is to find oneself in a situation that one has spent too much time and effort in and can’t get out of. Kendrick is claiming that there is even more to the situation about...
Ain't that somethin'? B-Rad stands for bitch and you Malibu most wanted
  > [#28, 269 votes] Kendrick references the movie Malibu’s Most Wanted, likening Drake to the main character Brad Gluckman, who attempts to emulate Black culture by becoming a rapper under the name “B-Rad”. Kendrick...
Ain't no law, boy, you ball boy, fetch Gatorade or somethin'
  > [#29, 150 votes] Kendrick Lamar is suggesting that Drake doesn’t hold any real authority or power. Instead, he refers to Drake as a “ball boy,” someone who typically assists athletes by fetching items like Gatorade...
Since 2009, I had this bitch jumpin'
  > [#30, 166 votes] Kendrick Lamar officially dropped the K-Dot moniker in 2009, upon the release of his self-titled EP. Ironically, on the tape’s final track, “Determined,” Kendrick says: She listenin' to Drake and all...
You niggas'll get a wedgie, be flipped over your boxers
What OVO for? The "Other Vaginal Option"? Pussy
  > [#31, 304 votes] Kendrick pokes fun at Drake’s record label, October’s Very Own (sometimes referred to as OVO Sound), in a similar manner to one of his previous diss tracks, “euphoria”: Yeah, OV-ho niggas is dick...
Nigga better straighten they posture, got famous all up in Compton
  > [#32, 80 votes] This line may be a dig at Drake’s producer and fellow crew member, OVO 40. OVO 40 is diagnosed with Multiple Sclerosis (MS), an autoimmune disease that affects the central nervous system. MS is known...
Might write this for the doctorate, tell the pop star quit hidin'
  > [#33, 125 votes] At the start of the beef after Kendrick was featured on “Like That”, Drake, the “POPSTAR” was the one trying to corral a response from Kendrick on “Push Ups” and “Taylor Made Freestyle”. You tryna...
Fuck a caption, want action, no accident
And I'm hands-on, he fuck around, get polished
  > [#34, 80 votes] Kendrick Lamar is continuing with a theme iterated on April 2024’s “euphoria,” when he said: If you take it there, I’m takin' it further Psst, that’s something you don’t wanna do For example,...
Fucked on Wayne girl while he was in jail, that's connivin'
  > [#35, 326 votes] While Lil Wayne—Drake’s mentor and then-label head—was imprisoned in 2010, Drake confessed to Wayne that he had slept with his then-partner, model/actress Tammy Torres. Tammy photographed separately...
Then get his face tatted like a bitch apologizin'
I'm glad DeRoz' came home, y'all didn't deserve him neither
  > [#36, 276 votes] Kendrick refers to DeMar DeRozan, also known as “De-Roz,” who is a professional basketball player and 6-time NBA all-star from Compton, California, Kendrick’s hometown. DeRozan played for Canada’s...
From Alondra down to Central, nigga better not speak on Serena
  > [#37, 275 votes] Kendrick warns Drake to not disrespect Serena Williams again, particularly in Los Angeles, as like Kendrick she was raised in Compton. Alondra Boulevard is a west–east thoroughfare and Central Avenue...
And your homeboy need subpoena, that predator move in flocks
  > [#38, 187 votes] Lamar doubles down on his allegation that Drake is a pedophile. Sex offenders are, via Megan’s Law, legally required to be publicly identifiable to their local community in the U.S. This line...
That name gotta be registered and placed on neighborhood watch
I lean on you niggas like another line of Wock'
  > [#39, 162 votes] Lean is a recreational drug made out of cough syrup, namely the Wockhardt brand. In the same way that Wockhardt cough syrup is literally in lean, Kendrick “leans on” or is propped up by the broader...
Yeah, it's all eyes on me, and I'ma send it up to Pac, ayy
  > [#40, 233 votes] Kendrick acknowledges that he’s in the spotlight, while making a nod to February 1996’s All Eyez On Me by 2Pac. Many, including Kendrick, found it distasteful when Drake replicated the late rapper’s...
Put the wrong label on me, I'ma get 'em dropped, ayy
Sweet Chin Music and I won't pass the aux, ayy
  > [#41, 279 votes] Kendrick Lamar makes reference to legendary WWE wrestler Shawn Michaels, whose signature finishing move is called “Sweet Chin Music.” The maneuver involves delivering a flat-footed kick to the...
How many stocks do I really have in stock? Ayy
  > [#42, 312 votes] Kendrick claims to have ten diss tracks. We’ve been exposed to five so far: “Like That” — March 22 “euphoria” — April 30 “6:16 in LA” — May 3 “meet the grahams” — May 3 “Not Like Us” — May 4 With the...
One, two, three, four, five, plus five, ayy
Devil is a lie, he a 69 God, ayy
Freaky-ass niggas need to stay they ass inside, ayy
  > [#43, 497 votes] The word “fan” doubles as an acronym for “freaky-ass nigga.” It can also be mentioned that Drake is being called a fan/spectator of the “black culture” rather than being a part of it. This is backed...
Roll they ass up like a fresh pack of 'za, ayy
  > [#44, 126 votes] “Za” or “zaza” is slang for marijuana or cannabis. Kendrick suggests that he can easily “smoke” or eliminate his opponents, like rolling up a joint or a blunt. Marijuana is commonly referenced within...
City is back up, it's a must, we outside, ayy

[Chorus]
They not like us, they not like us, they not like us
  > [#22, see above]
They not like us, they not like us, they not like us

[Verse 3]
Once upon a time, all of us was in chains
The settlers was usin' townfolk to make 'em richer
  > [#45, 417 votes] These lines are a response to Drake’s choice of words on May 2024’s “Family Matters”: Always rappin’ like you ’bout to get the slaves freed You just actin' like an activist, it’s make-believe Drake...
Homie still doubled down callin' us some slaves
Atlanta was the Mecca, buildin' railroads and trains
Bear with me for a second, let me put y'all on game
  > [#46, 47 votes] Kendrick Lamar might be alluding to The Game’s 2005 song “Put You on The Game,” as Drake mentioned Chuck Taylor—or Jayceon Terrell Taylor, The Game’s real name—on April 2024’s “Family Matters”: You...
Fast-forward, 2024, you got the same agenda
You run to Atlanta when you need a check balance
  > [#47, 145 votes] Kendrick claims Drake runs to Atlanta to collaborate with artists there, purely to make some money. As Kendrick discusses in the upcoming lines, this is typically done to increase Drake’s street...
Let me break it down for you, this the real nigga challenge
  > [#48, 71 votes] In 2018, Drake’s track “In My Feelings” was used for the mega-viral Kiki dance challenge, where people posted videos on social media of themselves jumping out of moving cars to dance to the song, set...
You called Future when you didn't see the club (Ayy, what?)
  > [#49, 189 votes] Kendrick claims Drake only collaborated with Future to get play in urban clubs. Future and Drake’s first collaboration came in 2011, on track “Tony Montana”. Around this time, Drake faced criticism...
Lil Baby helped you get your lingo up (What?)
  > [#50, 223 votes] Kendrick suggests that Drake collaborated and spent time with Lil Baby just to step his game up and that his usage of slang isn’t authentic. This is a doubling down on sentiments expressed in...
21 gave you false street cred
  > [#51, 236 votes] Drake has an extensive history of collaboration with 21 Savage—someone who is considered a bonafide gangster rapper. They’ve release several song’s since Drake’s October 2016 track, “Sneakin',” and...
Thug made you feel like you a slime in your head (Ayy, what?)
  > [#52, 249 votes] Thug has long been associated with slime, from lyrical references to rumors of Thug leading the Young Slime Street gang; Quavo sang about the Northside as a part of Migos; 2Chainz featured Drake on...
Quavo said you can be from Northside (What?)
2 Chainz say you good, but he lied
You run to Atlanta when you need a few dollars
  > [#53, 185 votes] Every rapper mentioned in previous lines in the song is from Atlanta, Georgia. Drake is often criticized for being a culture vulture, and Atlanta is one of the epicenters of hip-hop culture. Kendrick...
No, you not a colleague, you a fuckin' colonizer
  > [#54, 327 votes] The term “colonizer” identifies Drake’s relationship to hip hop and Black culture. Lamar uses the distinction between “colleague” and “colonizer” to indicate that Drake, who is half white, benefits...
The family matter and the truth of the matter
  > [#55, 339 votes] “Family Matters” is Drake’s response to Kendrick’s two tracks: “euphoria” and “6:16 in LA.” However “6:16 in LA,” “Family Matters” and “meet the grahams” were all published within a 24hr period....
It was God's plan to show y'all the liar

[Bridge]
Mm
Freaky-ass nigga, he a 69 God
  > [#43, see above]
Mm-mm
He a fan, he a fan, he a fan (Mm)
He a fan, he a fan, he a
Freaky-ass nigga, he a 69 God
Freaky-ass nigga, he a 69 God
Hey, hey, hey, hey, run for your life
Hey, hey, hey, hey, run for your life
  > [#56, 154 votes] “Run for Your Life” is a song by the English rock band the Beatles from their 1965 album Rubber Soul. The song’s lyrics establish a threatening tone towards the singer’s unnamed girlfriend, referred...
Hey, hey, hey, hey, run for your life
Freaky-ass nigga, he a 69 God
Hey, hey, hey, hey, run for your life
Let me hear you say, "OV-ho" (OV-ho)
  > [#57, 376 votes] Kendrick Lamar mocks Drake’s brand and label, OVO or October’s Very Own, with a catchy call-and-response. He originally referred to OVO as “OV-ho” on his first diss track directed at Drake, April...
Say, "OV-ho" (OV-ho)
Then step this way, step that way
  > [#58, 412 votes] Many fans believed Drake’s advantage in the two’s beef would be in creating a club-friendly diss track; few expected Kendrick Lamar to beat him to the punch. These simple dance instructions are...
Then step this way, step that way

[Outro]
Are you my friend?
  > [#58, see above]
Are we locked in?
Then step this way, step that way
Then step this way, step that way
